title: Alloparenting and Child Wellbeing
passage:
Parenting tips obtained from hunter-gatherers in Africa may be the key to bringing up more contented children, researchers have suggested. The idea is based on studies of communities such as the Kung of Botswana, where each child is cared for by many adults. Kung children as young as four will help to look after younger ones and baby-wearing, in which infants are carried in slings, is considered the norm.

According to Dr Nikhil Chaudhary, an evolutionary anthropologist at Cambridge University, these practices, known as alloparenting, could lead to less anxiety for children and parents.

Dr Annie Swanepoel, a child psychiatrist, believes that there are ways to incorporate them into western life. In Germany, one scheme has paired an old people's home with a nursery. The residents help to look after the children, an arrangement akin to alloparenting. Another measure could be encouraging friendships between children in different school years to mirror the unsupervised mixed-age playgroups in hunter-gatherer communities.

In a paper published in the Journal of Child Psychology and Psychiatry, researchers said that the western nuclear family was a recent invention which broke with evolutionary history. This abrupt shift to an intensive mothering narrative, which suggests that mothers should manage childcare alone, was likely to have been harmful. Such narratives can lead to maternal exhaustion and have dangerous consequences, they wrote.

By contrast, in hunter-gatherer societies adults other than the parents can provide almost half of a child's care. One previous study looked at the Efe people of the Democratic Republic of Congo. It found that infants had an average of 14 alloparents a day by the time they were 18 weeks old and were passed between caregivers eight times an hour.

Chaudhary said that parents now had less childcare support from family and social networks than during most of humans' evolutionary history, but introducing additional caregivers could reduce stress and maternal depression, which could have a knock-on benefit to a child's wellbeing. An infant born to a hunter-gatherer society could have more than ten caregivers. This contrasts starkly to nursery settings in the UK where regulations call for a ratio of one carer to four children aged two to three.

While hunter-gatherer children learnt from observation and imitation in mixed-age playgroups, researchers said that western instructive teaching, where pupils are asked to sit still, may contribute to conditions such as attention deficit hyperactivity disorder. Chaudhary said that Britain should explore the possibility that older siblings helping their parents might also enhance their own social development.

questions.questionText: According to the first two paragraphs, alloparenting refers to the practice of ________.
questions.question: According to the first two paragraphs, alloparenting refers to the practice of ________.

options.B: assigning babies to specific adult caregivers
options.C: teaching parenting details to older children
options.D: carrying infants around by their parent

questions.location: Each child is cared for by many adults ... these practices, known as alloparenting.
questions.explanation: 前两段先解释每个孩子由许多成人照看，随后把这种做法称为 alloparenting。
questions.thinkingMethod: 术语题要看定义前后句，而不是只看构词。
questions.trapAnalysis: B 的 specific caregivers 与 many adults 相反；C、D 只是局部现象。
questions.commonMistake: 范围缩小：把 alloparenting 理解成背婴儿或大孩子照看小孩这一个细节。

questions.questionText: The scheme in Germany is mentioned to illustrate ________.
questions.question: The scheme in Germany is mentioned to illustrate ________.

options.A: an attempt to facilitate intergenerational communication
options.B: an approach to integrating alloparenting into western culture
options.C: the conventional parenting style in western culture
options.D: the differences between western and African ways of living

questions.location: There are ways to incorporate them into western life. In Germany, one scheme...
questions.explanation: 德国项目是为了举例说明可以把 alloparenting 融入西方生活。
questions.thinkingMethod: 例证题向前找观点句：incorporate them into western life。
questions.trapAnalysis: A 只看到老人院与托儿所；C 与文章批判西方核心家庭相反；D 不是例子目的。
questions.commonMistake: 停留在表层例子：只看到老人和儿童互动，没回到 alloparenting 的论点。

questions.questionText: According to Paragraph 4, the intensive mothering narrative ________.
questions.question: According to Paragraph 4, the intensive mothering narrative ________.

options.D: departs from the course of evolution

questions.location: The western nuclear family was a recent invention which broke with evolutionary history.
questions.explanation: 第四段说西方核心家庭是近期发明，打破了进化历史；随后说母亲独自照护的叙事有害。
questions.thinkingMethod: 抓 broke with evolutionary history，对应 departs from the course of evolution。
questions.trapAnalysis: A 与 harmful、exhaustion 相反；B、C 原文未支持。
questions.commonMistake: 同义替换没识别：broke with evolutionary history 没看出等于 departs from evolution。

questions.questionText: According to Paragraph 6, what can we learn about nursery settings in the UK?
questions.question: According to Paragraph 6, what can we learn about nursery settings in the UK?

options.A: They tend to fall short of official requirements.
options.B: They have difficulty finding enough caregivers.
options.C: They ought to improve their carer-to-child ratio.
options.D: They should try to prevent parental depression.

questions.location: This contrasts starkly to nursery settings in the UK where regulations call for a ratio of one carer to four children.
questions.explanation: 作者用狩猎采集社会十多个照护者与英国托育 1:4 的比例强烈对比，暗示英国照护比例有改善空间。
questions.thinkingMethod: 推理题只比原文多走一步：从 stark contrast 推出比例需要改善。
questions.trapAnalysis: A 说不达官方要求，原文只是说官方要求是 1:4；B、D 原文没有直接推出。
questions.commonMistake: 过度推理：把 regulations call for 误解成托育机构达不到规定。

questions.questionText: Which of the following would be the best title?
questions.question: Which of the following would be the best title?

options.A: Instructive teaching: a dilemma for anxious parents
options.B: For a happier family, learn from the hunter-gatherers
options.C: Mixed-age playgroup, a better choice for lonely children
options.D: Tracing the history of parenting: from Africa to Europe

questions.location: Parenting tips obtained from hunter-gatherers in Africa may be the key to bringing up more contented children.
questions.explanation: 全文围绕从狩猎采集社会学习共同照护，以减少焦虑、改善儿童和家庭状态。
questions.thinkingMethod: 标题题要覆盖全文主线，不能只抓最后一段教学或混龄玩耍。
questions.trapAnalysis: A、C 都是局部内容；D 更像历史追溯，不能体现建议性主旨。
questions.commonMistake: 以偏概全：把 mixed-age playgroups 或 instructive teaching 当成全文中心。

vocabulary.sentence: Parenting tips may be the key to bringing up more contented children.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 这些育儿建议可能是培养更满足孩子的关键。
vocabulary.note: 与 anxious 形成语义对比。

vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 用背带携带婴儿被视为常态。
vocabulary.note: 考研阅读常用于社会习俗。

vocabulary.sentence: Dr Nikhil Chaudhary is an evolutionary anthropologist.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: Nikhil Chaudhary 博士是一名进化人类学家。

vocabulary.sentence: There are ways to incorporate them into western life.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 有办法把这些做法融入西方生活。
vocabulary.explanation: incorporate A into B 表示把 A 纳入 B。

vocabulary.sentence: The arrangement is akin to alloparenting.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 这种安排类似于共同育儿。
vocabulary.explanation: akin to 表示与某事相似。

vocabulary.sentence: This abrupt shift was likely to have been harmful.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 这种突然转变很可能是有害的。
vocabulary.explanation: abrupt 强调突然和缺少过渡。

vocabulary.sentence: Such narratives can lead to maternal exhaustion.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 这种叙事会导致母亲精疲力竭。

vocabulary.sentence: This contrasts starkly to nursery settings in the UK.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 这与英国托育环境形成鲜明对比。
vocabulary.explanation: stark contrast 表示强烈对比。

vocabulary.sentence: Children learnt from observation and imitation.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 孩子们通过观察和模仿学习。

vocabulary.sentence: Helping their parents might also enhance their own social development.
vocabulary.sentenceTranslation: 帮助父母也可能促进他们自身的社会性发展。
vocabulary.explanation: enhance 表示提高、增强积极效果。

articleStructure: 提出从狩猎采集社会学习育儿；定义 alloparenting；举德国例子说明西方可融入；批评 intensive mothering；对比多照护者模式；讨论英国托育和混龄学习启示。
